painStats.label: Bloodborne pathogens standard
painStats.sub: OSHA requires annual, employer-funded bloodborne pathogen training for every worker with reasonably anticipated exposure to blood or infectious materials, with documentation on file.

painStats.label: HIPAA training has no fixed hour count
painStats.sub: HHS confirms there is no single standardized HIPAA training program, so covered entities must document their own training was actually delivered and understood by each workforce member.

painStats.label: Clinical competencies expire quietly
painStats.sub: Certifications like BLS, ACLS, and unit-specific skills checkoffs lapse on a rolling basis, and a single expired credential found during a survey can trigger a deficiency citation.

faq: What training does OSHA require for healthcare workers?

OSHA's bloodborne pathogens standard, 29 CFR 1910.1030, requires initial and annual training for any employee with reasonably anticipated exposure to blood or other potentially infectious materials. isolved Learn & Grow assigns the module by job code and keeps a dated completion record for every employee.
Does HIPAA require a specific training program?

No. HHS confirms HIPAA training is flexible and scales to the organization, but you still must prove training happened and was understood. Learn & Grow tracks assignment, completion, and quiz scores so you can show an auditor exactly who was trained and when.

painStats.label: Fall protection trigger height
painStats.sub: Under OSHA 1926.501, any employee on a walking or working surface 6 feet or more above a lower level must be protected by a guardrail, safety net, or personal fall arrest system.

painStats.label: Most-cited OSHA violation
painStats.sub: Fall protection has topped OSHA's most-cited standards list for 15 straight years, with thousands of citations issued annually across the construction industry.

painStats.label: OSHA Outreach card levels
painStats.sub: Workers typically complete the 10-hour course for hazard awareness and supervisors complete the 30-hour course, and cards must be tracked so expired training does not put a crew on a jobsite unqualified.

solves.title: Prove compliance during a site inspection

faq: What training does OSHA require for construction workers?

OSHA 1926.501 requires fall protection for anyone working 6 feet or higher, and many employers layer on the OSHA 10-hour or 30-hour Outreach course for broader hazard awareness. isolved Learn & Grow assigns these by trade and tracks card expiration so a crew is never caught without current training.
Does the OSHA 10 or 30 card expire?

OSHA Outreach cards do not have a federal expiration, but many states, GCs, and unions require refreshers on a set cycle. Learn & Grow lets you set your own renewal schedule and get alerted before a worker's training falls out of your policy window.

manufacturing.hook: Skipping a lockout/tagout refresher is how a routine machine jam turns into a preventable amputation and an OSHA citation.

painStats.label: Lockout/tagout standard impact
painStats.sub: OSHA 1910.147 is estimated to prevent about 120 fatalities and 50,000 injuries a year by requiring trained, authorized employees to control hazardous energy before servicing equipment.

painStats.label: 1910.147 citation ranking
painStats.sub: Lockout/tagout ranked #4 on OSHA's most-cited standards in FY2025, with violations traced mostly to missing written procedures and inadequate employee training.

painStats.label: Required inspection and retraining cycle
painStats.sub: OSHA requires employers to conduct annual inspections of energy control procedures and retrain authorized employees whenever a new hazard, procedure change, or deviation is identified.

faq: What training does OSHA require for lockout/tagout?

OSHA 1910.147 requires a written energy control program, machine-specific procedures, and training for every authorized and affected employee, plus annual inspections. isolved Learn & Grow assigns the training by line and machine class and keeps proof ready for your annual audit.
How often does lockout/tagout training need to be repeated?

OSHA requires retraining whenever there is a job change, new equipment, a new hazard, or a deviation from procedure, on top of an annual program review. Learn & Grow tracks these triggers so retraining does not get missed between formal reviews.

painStats.sub: The ServSafe Food Handler certificate, aligned to the FDA Food Code, is valid for three years, and multi-unit operators need a system to know exactly which employee at which location is due for renewal.

painStats.label: Mandate allergen training
painStats.sub: California, Massachusetts, Maryland, Michigan, Rhode Island, and Virginia require restaurant employees or certified managers to complete food allergy awareness training, and requirements differ by state, which is hard to manage location by location without a system.

painStats.label: TIPS and food safety exam threshold
painStats.sub: Most alcohol server and food handler certifications require a passing score around 70 percent, and multi-unit brands need proof every location's staff actually passed, not just enrolled.

Most jurisdictions require food handlers to complete a food safety course aligned to the FDA Food Code, such as ServSafe, and require at least one certified food protection manager on staff. isolved Learn & Grow tracks certification status and expiration for every employee at every unit.
Do restaurants have to train staff on food allergies?

Six states, including California, Massachusetts, and Illinois, now require food allergy awareness training for restaurant staff or managers. Learn & Grow lets multi-unit operators assign the correct state-specific allergen module to each location and prove it was completed.

painStats.sub: States set their own dementia care training minimums for direct care staff, ranging from about 4 hours in some states to 16 hours in others, with separate annual continuing education on top.

painStats.label: Federal nurse aide training minimum
painStats.sub: Under 42 CFR 483.152, CMS requires nurse aide training and competency evaluation programs in Medicare and Medicaid-certified facilities to total at least 75 clock hours before certification.

painStats.label: Continuing education keeps resetting
painStats.sub: Most states require ongoing annual dementia and mental health de-escalation training on top of initial hours, and tracking that cycle manually across a growing staff is where operators fall behind.

faq: How many training hours does CMS require for nurse aides?

Federal regulation 42 CFR 483.152 requires at least 75 clock hours of training and a competency evaluation before a nurse aide can work unsupervised in a Medicare or Medicaid-certified facility. isolved Learn & Grow tracks progress toward those 75 hours and flags gaps before survey.
Is dementia care training required for assisted living staff?

Most states require a set number of initial dementia training hours plus annual continuing education for direct care staff, though the exact hours vary by state. Learn & Grow lets you configure the correct requirement per state and community and prove it on demand.

painStats.label: Federal home health aide training minimum
painStats.sub: 42 CFR 484.80 requires at least 75 hours of combined classroom and supervised practical training, including a minimum of 16 classroom hours before 16 hours of supervised practical work.

painStats.label: Required in-service training
painStats.sub: Every home health aide must complete at least 12 hours of in-service training during each 12-month period to keep furnishing services under Medicare conditions of participation.

painStats.label: Competency evaluation required
painStats.sub: An aide may only furnish services after successfully completing a competency evaluation, and the agency must maintain documentation proving the requirement was met before that first billable visit.

faq: How many hours of training does a home health aide need?

42 CFR 484.80 requires at least 75 hours of combined classroom and supervised practical training, with a minimum of 16 classroom hours preceding 16 hours of supervised practical training. isolved Learn & Grow tracks progress toward the full 75-hour requirement per aide.
Is ongoing training required after certification?

Yes. Home health aides must complete at least 12 hours of in-service training every 12 months to remain compliant with Medicare conditions of participation. Learn & Grow flags each aide's renewal window automatically so agencies do not miss the annual cycle.

painStats.sub: AICPA members must complete 120 hours of continuing professional education every three-year reporting period to maintain membership and demonstrate ongoing competency.

painStats.label: State board CPE ranges
painStats.sub: Each state board of accountancy sets its own CPE hour totals, reporting periods, and ethics minimums, so a multi-state firm must track different requirements for licensees in different jurisdictions.

painStats.label: Ethics hours required annually or per cycle
painStats.sub: Most state boards carve out a specific ethics CPE minimum within total hours, for example a set number of hours each renewal period, and missing it can jeopardize license renewal even if total hours are met.

faq: How many CPE hours does a CPA need?

AICPA members need 120 hours every three years, though each state board sets its own total, typically 80 to 120 hours per cycle, with a specific ethics hour minimum built in. isolved Learn & Grow tracks each CPA's hours against their specific state and cycle requirement.
Does ethics CPE have its own requirement?

Yes. Most state boards require a set number of ethics hours within the total CPE requirement each renewal period, and missing that carve-out can block license renewal even with enough total hours. Learn & Grow separates ethics completion from general CPE so nothing gets missed.

painStats.sub: The EPA's Agricultural Worker Protection Standard requires agricultural workers and pesticide handlers to receive WPS-specific training on their first day on the job and every 12 months after.

painStats.sub: Employers must maintain records of every WPS training session for at least two years following completion, a requirement that is hard to manage reliably on paper across seasonal crews.

painStats.label: Language and comprehension mandate
painStats.sub: EPA requires WPS training be delivered in a manner workers can understand, using a translator if necessary, which means tracking not just completion but the language it was delivered in.

faq: When must agricultural workers receive pesticide safety training?

EPA's Worker Protection Standard requires training on the first day on the job and every 12 months thereafter for workers and handlers with reasonably anticipated pesticide exposure. isolved Learn & Grow assigns the training automatically at hire and flags the annual renewal.
How long must WPS training records be kept?

EPA requires employers to retain training records for at least two years after completion. Learn & Grow stores every completion record digitally so it is ready the moment a state pesticide inspector asks.

AIA members need 18 learning units annually, with at least 12 in health, safety, and welfare topics, though each state licensing board sets its own separate requirement and cycle. isolved Learn & Grow tracks both AIA and state-specific hours per architect.

faq: What is Responsible Vendor Training for cannabis employees?

Most states require cannabis business agents to complete approved Responsible Vendor Training covering topics like preventing sales to minors and public health compliance, often required annually or within a set window after hire. isolved Learn & Grow tracks each employee's certificate and renewal deadline by state.
What happens if a cannabis employee's training lapses?

painStats.sub: Since a 1996 General Conference mandate, United Methodist churches and many other denominations require a documented child protection policy covering background checks, training, and reporting procedures for every congregation.

faq: What is Safe Sanctuaries training and who needs it?

Safe Sanctuaries is a child and vulnerable adult protection training, typically a 90-minute course with an assessment, required for any staff member or volunteer who works with children, youth, or vulnerable adults. isolved Learn & Grow assigns the course to every relevant volunteer and keeps a dated completion record.
How often should church volunteers get background checks?

Denominational guidance commonly recommends a national background check every three years for anyone supervising children or vulnerable adults. Learn & Grow tracks each volunteer's last check date and flags who is due for renewal.

education.hook: A teacher who misses a mandated reporter training requirement can face personal legal consequences, and so can the district that failed to document it.
education.metaTitle: School District Compliance Training Software | BEG
education.metaDesc: Assign mandated reporter, Title IX, and FERPA training, track completion by staff role, prove compliance instantly. See isolved Learn & Grow pricing.

painStats.label: Title IX responsible employee scope
painStats.sub: Any school employee who has authority to address harassment or a duty to report it is a Title IX responsible employee, meaning most staff, not just administrators, need documented training on reporting obligations.

painStats.label: Mandated reporter deadlines
painStats.sub: Teachers and staff acting as mandated reporters must report suspected abuse or neglect to authorities, often within a defined timeline set by state law, and failure to act can carry legal consequences for the individual educator.

painStats.stat: FERPA does not block Title IX duties
painStats.label: Two laws, one compliance record
painStats.sub: FERPA protects student education records, but it does not restrict a school's duty to prevent and address discrimination under Title IX, meaning staff need training on how both rules interact, not just one in isolation.

solves.title: Assign mandated reporter and Title IX training by role
solves.body: isolved Learn & Grow lets a district push mandated reporter, Title IX responsible employee, and FERPA training to every staff category, with documented completion for each.

solves.title: Track annual and new-hire completion deadlines
solves.body: Get automatic reminders as new staff onboard and as annual refresher deadlines approach for existing employees across every building in the district.

solves.title: Prove compliance to your school board or state auditor
solves.body: Generate a district-wide or building-specific training completion report instantly when a board member, state auditor, or Title IX coordinator needs documentation.

faq: Who is required to complete mandated reporter training in schools?

Most states require teachers, paraprofessionals, and other school staff to act as mandated reporters of suspected abuse or neglect, with training and reporting timelines set by state law. isolved Learn & Grow assigns the training to every applicable role and documents completion for state compliance.
Does FERPA prevent schools from reporting under Title IX?

No. FERPA protects student education records, but it does not restrict a school's duty to report and address discrimination under Title IX. Learn & Grow lets districts train staff on both requirements separately so responsibilities under each law are clear and documented.

OSHA treats staffing agencies and host employers as joint employers for safety training purposes, generally expecting the agency to cover generic safety training and the client to cover site-specific hazards. isolved learn and grow tracks completion of both so you can show your side of the record.
Can we track different training requirements for the same worker across different placements?
Yes. You can assign and track training per placement, so a worker rotating between client sites has a current, documented record specific to each assignment.

technology.hook: Security awareness training that shows up as evidence in your SOC 2 report, not just a slide deck memory
technology.metaTitle: SOC 2 Security Awareness Training Software | BEG
technology.metaDesc: Assign annual security awareness, privacy, and code-of-conduct training, track completion for every employee, and produce SOC 2 audit evidence. isolved learn and grow.

painStats.label: Completion rate auditors expect for SOC 2 training evidence
painStats.sub: SOC 2 Type II auditors look for an annual training completion report showing full workforce coverage, not a sample, across the entire audit observation period.

painStats.label: Typical window for new-hire security training
painStats.sub: Best practice, and what most SOC 2 auditors expect, is that new employees complete security awareness training within their first 30 days, ideally before production access is granted.

painStats.label: How often gaps in training evidence get discovered
painStats.sub: A SOC 2 Type II audit reviews training completion throughout the entire period, so a single quarter with no documented refresher training becomes a finding.

solves.title: Assign annual security awareness training company-wide
solves.body: isolved learn and grow lets you require and refresh security awareness, data privacy, and code-of-conduct training for every employee, from engineering to sales.

solves.title: Track new-hire onboarding against the 30-day window
solves.body: Automated tracking flags new hires approaching their onboarding training deadline, keeping you ahead of the completion window auditors expect.

solves.title: Generate SOC 2 audit-ready completion reports
solves.body: Pull a full-workforce, dated completion report covering the entire audit period, exactly what your SOC 2 auditor requests as training evidence.

faq: What training evidence does a SOC 2 Type II audit actually require?

Auditors expect a documented, dated record showing all employees completed annual security awareness training throughout the observation period, not just at one point in time. isolved learn and grow generates that full-workforce completion report on demand.
Can we track new-hire security training separately from annual refreshers?
Yes. You can assign onboarding security training with its own due date for every new hire, then automatically roll them into the annual refresher cycle once onboarding is complete.

trades.hook: OSHA 10, OSHA 30, and licensing CE tracked by crew, so the job site is never one card short
trades.metaTitle: OSHA 10/30 and Trades Training Tracking | BEG
trades.metaDesc: Assign OSHA 10, OSHA 30, and state licensing CE to every crew member, track card renewals, and prove job-site compliance. isolved learn and grow, national support.

painStats.label: New York City's deadline for a new hire's OSHA 10 card
painStats.sub: NYC requires every construction employee to obtain an OSHA 10-hour card within 15 days of hire, and supervisory staff need OSHA 30 on the same timeline.

painStats.label: Public project threshold triggering mandatory OSHA 10
painStats.sub: Many jurisdictions require OSHA 10-hour training for every worker on public projects estimated above $10,000, a threshold that catches even small subcontractor crews.

painStats.label: Who needs OSHA 30 on most job sites
painStats.sub: OSHA 30 is generally required or recommended for any worker with supervisory or safety responsibility on a job site, distinct from the base OSHA 10 requirement for the rest of the crew.

solves.title: Assign OSHA 10 and OSHA 30 by role
solves.body: isolved learn and grow lets you require OSHA 10 for every crew member and OSHA 30 for supervisors, matching the jurisdiction and project requirements you actually work under.

solves.title: Track state trade licensing CE alongside safety cards
solves.body: Electricians, plumbers, and HVAC techs often carry separate state licensing CE requirements. Track those renewal cycles in the same system as OSHA cards.

solves.title: Prove compliance before a job site inspection
solves.body: Pull a crew-by-crew report showing current OSHA cards and licensing CE, ready before a general contractor or municipal inspector asks for it.

authority.label: OSHA, Outreach Training Program for construction

faq: Do all our crew members need OSHA 10, or just supervisors?

OSHA 10 is generally expected for all construction workers where required by state or local law, while OSHA 30 is aimed at supervisors and anyone with safety responsibility. isolved learn and grow assigns the right course by role and tracks card status by crew.
Can we track state trade licensing CE hours in the same system as OSHA cards?
Yes. You can track electrician, plumbing, and HVAC continuing education requirements alongside OSHA 10/30 cards, so every worker's full compliance picture is in one place.

trucking.hook: ELDT, hours-of-service, and hazmat training documented before FMCSA asks, not after
trucking.metaTitle: FMCSA ELDT and Driver Training Software | BEG
trucking.metaDesc: Assign Entry Level Driver Training, hazmat, and annual safety training to every driver, track completion, and prove FMCSA compliance. isolved learn and grow.

painStats.label: Date ELDT became mandatory for new CDL holders
painStats.sub: FMCSA's Entry-Level Driver Training rule requires anyone obtaining a CLP on or after that date to complete both theory and behind-the-wheel training before earning a Class A or Class B CDL.

painStats.label: Hazmat employee training renewal cycle
painStats.sub: Under 49 CFR 172.704, hazmat employees must be retrained at least once every 3 years, covering general awareness, function-specific, safety, and security awareness training.

painStats.label: Grace period for a new hazmat employee to complete training
painStats.sub: A new hazmat employee can perform job functions before training is finished only if it is completed within 90 days of hire or a change in job function.

solves.title: Track ELDT completion before CDL testing
solves.body: isolved learn and grow documents each new driver's theory and behind-the-wheel Entry-Level Driver Training completion, satisfying the FMCSA Training Provider Registry record requirement.

solves.title: Track hazmat retraining on the 3-year cycle
solves.body: Automated tracking flags hazmat-endorsed drivers approaching their 49 CFR 172.704 retraining deadline, and flags new hires against the 90-day grace period.

solves.title: Prove annual safety training during a DOT audit
solves.body: Generate a per-driver training and certification record covering ELDT, hazmat, and ongoing safety training, ready for an FMCSA compliance review.

faq: Do we need to document ELDT completion for every new driver hired after February 2022?

Yes. FMCSA requires anyone obtaining a commercial learner's permit on or after February 7, 2022 to complete Entry-Level Driver Training before CDL testing. isolved learn and grow tracks each driver's theory and behind-the-wheel completion for your records.
How often does hazmat training actually need to be renewed?

Under 49 CFR 172.704, hazmat employees must be retrained at least once every 3 years, and new hires get a 90-day grace period. isolved learn and grow tracks each driver's due date against that cycle automatically.

veterinary.hook: Hazardous drug handling and controlled substance training documented the way OSHA and DEA expect
veterinary.metaTitle: Veterinary Practice Safety Training Software | BEG
veterinary.metaDesc: Assign OSHA hazardous drug, radiation safety, and DEA controlled-substance training to every staff member, track completion, and prove compliance. isolved learn and grow.

painStats.label: Hazardous drugs veterinary staff regularly handle
painStats.sub: NIOSH includes veterinary care workers among those who handle hazardous drugs, and OSHA guidance on safe handling applies directly to veterinary practices administering chemotherapy and other agents.

painStats.label: Recommended refresher for hazardous drug and PPE training
painStats.sub: Staff handling hazardous drugs, sharps, and animal restraint should receive both initial and annual refresher training on safe handling and PPE use.

painStats.label: Requirement for any practice storing controlled substances
painStats.sub: Practices storing and dispensing controlled substances need proper DEA registration and recordkeeping, and staff handling those substances need documented training on storage and record accuracy.

solves.title: Assign hazardous drug and PPE training by role
solves.body: isolved learn and grow lets you require OSHA-aligned hazardous drug handling, sharps safety, and PPE training for every clinical staff member, refreshed annually.

solves.title: Track radiation safety and DEA training separately
solves.body: Assign radiation safety training for staff operating imaging equipment and controlled-substance handling training for anyone with DEA-related recordkeeping responsibility.

solves.title: Prove compliance during a state board or OSHA inspection
solves.body: Generate a dated, per-employee training record covering hazardous drugs, radiation safety, and controlled substances, ready for any inspection or audit.

authority.label: Common OSHA requirements in veterinary facilities

faq: Does OSHA guidance on hazardous drugs actually apply to veterinary practices?

Yes. NIOSH specifically includes veterinary care workers among those who handle hazardous drugs, since many drugs used in human medicine, including chemotherapy agents, are also used in animal care. isolved learn and grow lets you assign and track that training for every clinical staff member.
Can we track DEA controlled-substance training separately from general safety training?

Yes. You can build a separate required track for controlled-substance storage and recordkeeping training, alongside hazardous drug and radiation safety training, all tracked by employee in one system.
